helpers.name: reserve_type

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: File ID where type is defined

parameters.name: skip_id_naming
parameters.description: If true, skip ID embedding in name. Sets id_in_name=0 in database.

helpers.purpose: Reserve a type ID before writing the definition. Include definition_json and description — null fields force full file reads in future sessions.
return_statements: Use returned ID for type naming: {TypeName}_id_{id}

MANDATORY: Include definition_json (full type shape) AND description (what it represents, 5-10 words). Types are the MOST frequently looked-up entities — they are how AI understands data shapes without reading files. Incomplete type entries force full file reads that the DB should eliminate. Example: definition_json={'kind': 'union', 'variants': [{'kind': 'handle', 'handle': 'Handle'}, {'kind': 'direct', 'address': 'string'}]}, description='Payment recipient — handle or direct address'
Next: write type definition with ID in name → finalize_type(type_id, name, definition_json)

used_by_directives.description: Reserve type ID before writing code - AI embeds ID in type name for instant lookups (TypeName_id_7)
Reserve type IDs before generating type code - enables rename-proof type references

implementation_notes: Returns {success: true, id: reserved_id, is_reserved: true, id_in_name: !skip_id_naming}
SQL: INSERT INTO types (name, definition_json, description, links, file_id, is_reserved, id_in_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?) RETURNING id
Type name pattern: {TypeName}_id_{id} (e.g., Maybe_id_7) unless skip_id_naming=true
If skip_id_naming=true: id_in_name=0, name stays as-is

helpers.name: reserve_types

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Array of type objects [{name, definition_json, description, links, file_id, skip_id_naming?}, ...]

helpers.purpose: Batch-reserve type IDs before writing multiple type definitions. Include definition_json and description for every type.
return_statements: Use returned IDs for type naming: {TypeName}_id_{id}

MANDATORY: Include definition_json (full type shape) AND description for EVERY type. Types are the MOST frequently looked-up entities — they are how AI understands data shapes without reading files. Incomplete type entries force full file reads that the DB should eliminate.
Next: write type definitions with IDs in names → finalize_types([{type_id, name, definition_json}, ...])
implementation_notes: Returns {success: true, ids: [id1, id2, ...]} in same order as input types array
SQL: Multiple INSERT INTO types (name, definition_json, description, links, file_id, is_reserved, id_in_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?) (in transaction)
IDs correspond to types array indices: types[0] gets ids[0], types[1] gets ids[1], etc.
Each item has its own skip_id_naming flag - allows mixed batch with some types having IDs, some not
id_in_name is set to !skip_id_naming for each type individually

helpers.name: finalize_type

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: File ID where type is defined

parameters.name: skip_id_naming
parameters.description: If true, skip _id_XX pattern validation (for types where ID naming was skipped during reservation)

return_statements: WARNING: Verify definition_json and description are populated. Types with null description are incomplete tracking entries — future AI sessions cannot understand the type shape from the DB. If description is null, populate it NOW via update_type(). This is your last chance before the type is considered 'tracked'.
Do any type-function relationships need to be added in types_functions table?
If called during project_reserve_finalize: follow project_update_db directive to sync type registrations.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, type_id: int, file_id: int}
Sets is_reserved=0 in types table
Calls update_file_timestamp(file_id) automatically
Definition_json stores ADT structure for FP analysis
Name validation: must contain '_id_{type_id}' pattern UNLESS skip_id_naming=true

used_by_directives.description: Finalize type after code written - sets is_reserved=false and updates file timestamp
Finalize type reservations after successful code generation
Finalize type reservations when updating database with new type metadata

helpers.name: finalize_types

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Array of type objects [{type_id, name, definition_json, description, links, file_id, skip_id_naming?}, ...]

return_statements: WARNING: Verify definition_json and description are populated for ALL types. Types with null description are incomplete tracking entries — future AI sessions cannot understand type shapes from the DB. If any description is null, populate it NOW via update_type(). This is your last chance before types are considered 'tracked'.
Do any type-function relationships need to be added in types_functions table?
If called during project_reserve_finalize: follow project_update_db directive to sync type registrations.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, finalized_ids: [int, ...]}
Groups types by file_id for efficient timestamp updates
Sets is_reserved=0 for all types in batch
Each item has its own skip_id_naming flag - validation is per-item, not global
If skip_id_naming=true for an item, skip _id_XX pattern validation for that specific type

helpers.name: update_type
return_statements: Ensure name or definition changes are updated throughout codebase
If name changed, verify _id_xxx suffix is retained
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, type_id: int, file_id: int}
NULL parameters = don't update that field
Calls update_file_timestamp(file_id) automatically

used_by_directives.description: Update type metadata after code generation - synchronizes type definitions and ADT structures
Update stale type metadata during DB-filesystem sync - fixes outdated type definitions

helpers.name: delete_type
helpers.error_handling: Return error if types_functions exist - requires manual unlinking first
return_statements: Ensure the type code is also removed from the file, not just the database
implementation_notes: ERROR LOGIC: Query types table for is_reserved WHERE id=? - if is_reserved=1, return error
ERROR RETURN (reserved): {success: false, error: 'type_reserved', message: 'Cannot delete reserved type. Use delete_reserved helper or finalize_type first'}
ERROR LOGIC: Query types_functions WHERE type_id=? - if not empty, return error with function relationships list
ERROR RETURN: {success: false, error: 'types_functions_exist', function_relationships: [{function_id, function_name, role}, ...]}
ERROR MESSAGE: 'Cannot delete type - function relationships exist. Delete types_functions entries first, then retry'
SUCCESS LOGIC: Only proceed if is_reserved=0 AND types_functions check returns empty
SUCCESS LOGIC: Get file_id before deletion: SELECT file_id FROM types WHERE id=type_id
SUCCESS LOGIC: Create note entry in notes table with reason, severity, source, type, reference details
SUCCESS LOGIC: Delete type: DELETE FROM types WHERE id=type_id
SUCCESS RETURN: {success: true, deleted_type_id: type_id, file_id: file_id}
NOTE: types_functions must be manually unlinked first

used_by_directives.description: Cascade delete all types in file - automatically called when file is deleted

helpers.name: get_type_by_name
helpers.purpose: Look up a tracked type by name with full definition and file location — use BEFORE reading source files. Returns definition_json and description.
helpers.error_handling: Return error if no types found with that name
return_statements: DB-FIRST: Use this lookup BEFORE reading source files. Type definitions (definition_json, description) in the DB provide the full type shape — often eliminating the need to read the file. Only read source files when the DB doesn't have what you need.

used_by_directives.description: Look up types by name during DB-filesystem sync - verifies type entries exist before syncing
Query types by name for dependency graph generation - maps type relationships

implementation_notes: Returns array of type objects with file data (multiple types can share same name across files)
SQL: SELECT t.*, fi.name AS file_name, fi.path AS file_path FROM types t LEFT JOIN files fi ON t.file_id = fi.id WHERE t.name = ?
Returns: {success, types: [{id, name, file_id, file_name, file_path, definition_json, description, links, is_reserved, id_in_name, created_at, updated_at}, ...]}
Returns TypeQueryResult with types tuple

helpers.name: add_types_functions

parameters.name: relationships
parameters.description: Array of tuples [(type_id, function_id, role), ...] - role: 'factory', 'transformer', 'operator', 'pattern_matcher', 'accessor', 'validator', 'combinator'

helpers.purpose: Link tracked types to the functions that use them with a role (factory, transformer, validator, etc.). Enables 'what functions use TypeX?' queries.
return_statements: Verify all functions that consume or produce tracked types have been linked. Missing type-function relationships make 'what functions use TypeX?' queries incomplete — the AI loses refactoring impact analysis for that type.

Role auto-inference guide: returns type only → 'factory'; params AND returns same type → 'transformer'; params with boolean/Result return → 'validator'; params only → 'accessor' or 'operator'. For complex cases: 'pattern_matcher' (destructures type variants), 'combinator' (combines multiple instances of type). Correct auto-inferred roles if needed via update_type_function_role.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, ids: [int, ...]} - inserted relationship IDs
For single relationship: pass [(type_id, function_id, role)]
Roles help FP analysis understand type usage patterns
Inserts into types_functions table

used_by_directives.description: Track type-function relationships after code generation - creates relationship records for factory/transformer/accessor functions

helpers.name: update_type_function_role
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
Updates role field only in types_functions table
For type_id/function_id changes: delete + add new

helpers.name: delete_type_function
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
Deletes from types_functions table
Creates note record for audit trail

helpers.name: add_interaction
helpers.purpose: Record a dependency between two functions by name (call, chain, borrow, compose, pipe). Interactions are how the AI traces dependency graphs.
return_statements: Verify all function calls in code are tracked in the interactions table
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, id: int} - new interaction ID
Inserts into interactions table
Source/target are function names - resolved to IDs internally via JOIN with files table

DISAMBIGUATION: If function name matches multiple functions, returns error with list of matches [{id, name, file_name, file_path}] — AI should choose the correct one and call add_interactions with specific function IDs instead
If exactly 1 match: proceeds normally. If 0 matches: returns 'not found' error

used_by_directives.description: Track function dependencies after code generation - creates interaction records for function relationships

helpers.name: add_interactions

parameters.name: interactions
parameters.description: Array of tuples [(source_function_id, target_function_id, interaction_type, description), ...] - type: 'call', 'chain', 'borrow', 'compose', 'pipe'

helpers.purpose: Batch-record function dependencies by ID. More efficient than multiple add_interaction calls for tracking all cross-function dependencies.
return_statements: Verify all function calls in code are tracked in the interactions table
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, ids: [int, ...]} - inserted interaction IDs
Uses function IDs (not names)
More efficient than multiple single-interaction calls

used_by_directives.description: Batch track function dependencies after code generation - more efficient for multiple interactions

helpers.name: update_interaction
return_statements: Ensure the code reflects this interaction change
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
NULL parameters = don't update that field
Updates interactions table

used_by_directives.description: Update stale interaction metadata during DB-filesystem sync - fixes outdated function dependencies

helpers.name: delete_interaction
return_statements: Ensure the function dependency/call has been removed from the code, not just the database
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
Deletes from interactions table
Creates note record for audit trail

helpers.name: get_interactions_by_function

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: Function name to look up interactions for

helpers.purpose: Get all interactions for a function (both as source and target) — trace dependency graphs
return_statements: DB-FIRST: Use this to trace dependency graphs BEFORE reading source files. Shows what a function calls (source) and what calls it (target) — often answers investigation questions without reading code.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success, interactions: [{id, source_function_id, source_function_name, source_file_name, source_file_path, target_function_id, target_function_name, target_file_name, target_file_path, interaction_type, description, created_at, updated_at}, ...]}
SQL JOINs interactions with functions and files tables to resolve names and paths
Returns both directions: interactions where function is source AND where function is target
Interaction types: 'call', 'chain', 'borrow', 'compose', 'pipe'
Empty interactions tuple (not error) if function exists but has no interactions

used_by_directives.description: Trace function dependency graph during investigation — query interactions before reading source files

helpers.name: get_interactions_by_file

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: File ID to look up interactions for

helpers.purpose: Get all interactions for functions in a file — understand file's dependency role
return_statements: DB-FIRST: Use this to understand a file's dependencies BEFORE reading it. Shows all function relationships involving this file — often answers 'how is this file connected?' without reading source code.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success, interactions: [{id, source_function_id, source_function_name, source_file_name, source_file_path, target_function_id, target_function_name, target_file_name, target_file_path, interaction_type, description, created_at, updated_at}, ...]}
SQL JOINs interactions with functions and files tables to resolve names and paths
Returns interactions where ANY function in the file is source OR target
Interaction types: 'call', 'chain', 'borrow', 'compose', 'pipe'
Empty interactions tuple (not error) if file has functions but no interactions

used_by_directives.description: Trace file-level dependency graph during investigation — query all interactions for functions in a file

metadata.description: Type reservation/finalization, type-function relationships, and interaction management
